candidates.why: Generic top-line revenue label; likely primary sales measure and should be in the REVT priority list.
Explicit revenue from contracts with customers, which is typically the core operating revenue; best semantic match for REVT.
A technical balancing line, not an economic revenue measure; should not be used for REVT if proper revenue lines exist.
Combines revenue with other income; less ideal than pure revenue lines for REVT, but could serve as a fallback if needed.

variables.notes: Use contract revenue as primary REVT; fall back to generic Revenue if missing in some years.

candidates.row_label: Cost of Goods and Services Sold
candidates.why: Explicit cost of goods and services sold; direct production/service costs and correct mapping for COGS.
Balance sheet inventory level, not a period cost; should not be used as COGS when an income statement COGS line exists.

final_choice.row_label: Cost of Goods and Services Sold

candidates.why: Aggregate operating expenses bucket likely including COGS and SG&A; per instructions, prefer component rows over this total.
Classic SG&A-type overhead bucket (other operating expenses) excluding COGS and D&A; appropriate SG&A component.
Personnel/employee costs, typically part of SG&A; fits overhead definition and should be included as SG&A component.
Income line, not an operating expense; should be excluded from SG&A components.

variables.notes: Exclude the total 'Operating Expenses' and the income line 'Other Operating Income'; use the two explicit overhead components.

candidates.row_label: Amortization excluding Goodwill in Research & Development Expenses
candidates.why: Amortization component related to R&D, but not the total R&D expense itself; less suitable than the direct R&D expense line.
Explicit R&D expense line in the income statement; best match for period R&D expense (XRD).
Capitalized portion of R&D, typically reducing expensed R&D; not the pure expense line to be mapped to XRD.
Balance sheet asset from capitalized development costs; not a period expense.
Balance sheet gross R&D asset; not the income statement expense.

variables.notes: Use the explicit income statement R&D expense line; ignore capitalized and balance sheet amounts for XRD.

candidates.why: Net of interest income and expense; less preferred than pure interest expense lines but can serve as a fallback if needed.
Primary gross interest expense line; best match for XINT.
Interest component of lease liabilities; a specific type of interest expense that should be in the XINT fallback list.
Other interest expenses; complementary to main interest expense and suitable as additional fallback.

candidates.row_label: Interest expense on financial liabilities measured at fair value
candidates.why: Specific category of interest expense; appropriate as a lower-priority fallback for XINT.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expenses on Capital Financing, Gross
candidates.why: Label suggests gross capital financing interest, but appears unpopulated in preview; could be a legacy or unused line.
Capitalized interest, not period expense; should not be used for XINT as defined.
Represents minority interest share of income, not interest expense; should not be mapped to XINT.
Income allocation to non-controlling interests, not interest expense.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental disclosure of lease interest; can be a lower-priority fallback for XINT.
Interest-earning asset, not an expense; inappropriate for XINT.
Interest-bearing receivables asset; not an interest expense.

candidates.row_label: Current Non Interest-bearing Receivables, Related Parties
candidates.why: Non-interest-bearing receivable; irrelevant for XINT.
Equity for non-controlling interests, not interest expense.
Minority equity, not interest expense.

final_choice.row_label: Interest expense on financial liabilities measured at fair value
Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental

variables.notes: Ordered to prefer the main gross interest expense, then other and specific categories, with net interest and supplemental lines as further fallbacks.

candidates.why: Income from equity-accounted investees, not the book value of equity; not suitable for BE.
P&L gain/loss, not equity balance.

candidates.row_label: Equity holders of the parent company
candidates.why: Refers to income or comprehensive income attributable to equity holders, not the equity stock; less appropriate for BE.

candidates.row_label: Translation differences related to equity accounted investees
candidates.why: OCI component, not equity total.

candidates.row_label: Change in fair value of equity investments over OCI
candidates.why: OCI remeasurement, not total equity.

candidates.row_label: Change in fair value of equity investments
candidates.why: Unclear but appears to be a revaluation item, not equity total.
Investment in associates/joint ventures, not shareholders’ equity.

candidates.row_label: Total equity attributable to the parent
candidates.why: Direct measure of shareholders’ equity (book equity) attributable to parent; ideal for BE.
Total equity including non-controlling interests; acceptable but slightly less precise for parent-only book equity than previous line.

final_choice.row_label: Total equity attributable to the parent

variables.notes: Use parent equity as primary BE; total equity including minorities as a fallback.

candidates.why: Income statement allocation to minority interests; useful for flows but MIB here targets balance sheet minority equity when available.
Income statement non-controlling interest share; similar to Minority Interests but not the balance sheet stock.
Balance sheet line for non-controlling interests; a correct representation of minority equity (MIB).
Alternative label for balance sheet minority equity with identical values; best primary choice for MIB as explicit minority interest equity.

candidates.row_label: Comprehensive Income before Minority Interest - Total
candidates.why: Total comprehensive income before allocation to minorities; not a balance sheet minority interest value.

variables.notes: Use balance sheet minority equity; keep both labels in priority order in case of label changes across years.
